Recommended reading

Innovation - Business focus:

[Marr, B. (2021) Business trends in Practice, Wiley (Chapters 14 & 15)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166) – available on VLeBooks

Innovation - NLP focus:

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 4) – available via Kortext

It is strongly advised that you read the recommended module reading list to ensure you have covered the learning outcomes fully. However, it is not compulsory to read everything. 

From <[https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-5-streaming-trends-and-machine-translation-1698/?context_id=15599&context_resource=lp](https://app.qa.com/resource/topic-5-streaming-trends-and-machine-translation-1698/?context_id=15599&context_resource=lp)>

# Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 4)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Different people might translate a sentence various ways, so there are difference references,

Reference = {reference1, reference2…}

The output sequence is the reference.

2014 WMT contains several European language datasets. Some from European parliament proceedings parallel corpus.

Out of Vocabulary(OOV), is when you remove words that aren't commonly used, stuff like misspelled words, abbreviations ,etc.

In training you go from sentence to reference

The model should produce a candidate that matches the reference

Bilingual Evaluation Understudy Score (BLEU) provides a method to evaluate the translations

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Smoothing techniques add more uncertainty, but makes models more accurate. In terms of the score they give is better.

[Google Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/app)

- Machine translation has now exceeded human baselines. (False)
- Machine translation requires large datasets. (True)
- There is no need to compare transformer models using the same datasets. (False)
- BLEU is the French word for blue and is the acronym of an NLP metric. (False)
- Smoothing techniques enhance BERT. (False)
- German-English is the same as English-German for machine translation. (False)
- The Original Transformer multi-head attention sub-layer has two heads. (False)
- The Original Transformer encoder has six layers. (True)
- The Original Transformer encoder has six layers but only two decoder layers. (False)
- You can train transformers without decoders. (True)

# Main

## Technology as a service

- Easy access
- Scalability
- Reduced upfront costs
- Increased security

Robotics as a service(RaaS) includes physical or robotic process automation

AI as a service

Subscription box

## Direct to consumer

Intermediation - intermediate that's not the primary producer

Disintermediation - removing intermediates in the supply chain

Direct-to-consumer(disintermediation) is increasing.

e.g. Disney+

Augmented reality(AR) use to see what glasses would look like

### Journal

Hello fresh is a subscription food service

Adobe suit is subscription only, and it's a minimum of a year. It's a way to make more money at the expense of customer cost/experience.

Spotify is a great example of where a subscription service which benefits customers. Relatively cheap and very useful. There is disintermediation since it removes the record shop.

Media companies are mainly impacted by disintermediation

## Human and machine translation

Transduction comes up often in NLP. Learn from examples rather than creating functions. A transducer in electronics might be a microphone which converts speech into an electronic signal. Similar concept in AI.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

With transduction there is no approximating function

The output sequence is known as a the reference rather than prediction.

## Evaluating machine translations

WMT 2014 is a collection of datasets of Ninth Workshop on Statistical Machine Translation.

1. A new translation task
2. A quality estimation task
3. A metrics task
4. A medical text translation task

Bilingual Evaluation Understudy Score(BLEU) compares the candidate sentence(C) to a reference sentence® or several reference sentences.

C(N) number of correct tokens

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Later they smoothed things.

e.g. candidate_words = [drinks, likes, enjoys, appreciates]

Softmax = [0.7,.1,.1,.1]

One_hot=[1,0,0,0]

Can apply smoothing epsilon

![](data:image/png;base64,)

Smooth = [0.0830.75, 0.083, 0.083, 0.083]

## Google translate

Google translate platform -

translation api,

media translation api that can translate streaming content.

AutoML translation service, will train a custom model for a specific domain

Google Brain developed Tensor2Tensor T2T, an TensorFlow extension that contains a library of deep learning models, that contain many transformer examples.

Google brain then produced Trax, an end-to-end deep learning library. Uses TensorFlow and JAX under the hood.

Trax has lots of methods that can be applied to get it running properly

### Journal

Google translate can translate images from different languages

Recent stuff translating handsign language

Translate words to captions on films/shows

Machine translation is done sentence by sentence

Transduction is used rather than induction or deduction

Data needs to be prepared line by line

BLEU is a method for evaluating translations.

Google has made great progress with statistical machine translation

Google Trax is a product that aids in the application of transformer models

Recommended reading

[Marr, B. (2021) Business trends in Practice, Wiley (Chapters 14 & 15)](https://www.vlebooks.com/Product/Index/2705166) – available on VLeBooks

Rothman, D. (2024) Transformers for Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision, Packt, Third Edition (Chapter 4) – available via Kortext
